body:
Megan,

Still waiting on the follow-up you promised regarding the Wake Up alert delay. It fired twelve minutes late this morning — which doesn't sound like much until you're the one who missed her riverwalk window and arrived at Main Street already behind.

I run three locations. The 5:15 alert exists so I can get the walk done, grab my latte, and still hit opening prep by 8:30. When it slips, everything downstream slips. Doors open at 9:00 regardless.

I need a confirmed fix date, not another 'we're looking into it.'

Cecilia Burnett
Golden Prairie Nails

Cecilia,

Thank you for following up — and I apologize that the delay persisted after our last exchange. I've pulled your account logs and confirmed the notification trigger is miscalibrated on our end, not a device issue. Our engineering team pushed a patch this afternoon that should resolve the timing drift entirely.

To confirm: your Wake Up alert is set for 5:15 AM Central. Starting tomorrow morning it should fire within a 60-second window of that time. If it misses by more than two minutes, reply directly to this thread and I'll escalate to Priority tier immediately — no queue.

I know your mornings run on a tight sequence. This should be fixed.

Megan Burnett
Customer Support — Wake Up
direct@wakeupapp.com

Megan,

Doing the riverwalk most mornings before I open Main Street — 6:15 CT, roughly 35 minutes. Been trying to make it actual meditation instead of just walking while mentally drafting a schedule. Not the same thing, it turns out.

Looking for an audio-only guided walking meditation. No screen-staring, no subscription that costs more than it's worth. Craig suggested I "just breathe and be present," which is a lovely idea from someone who has never juggled three salon locations before 9 a.m.

What do you have?

— Cecilia

Hi Cecilia,

Thanks for reaching out! For audio-only guided walking meditation, Insight Timer is the strongest free option — large library, walking-specific sessions from 10 to 45 minutes, and no screen interaction once you hit play. The body-scan-while-walking format tends to work well for people who are used to moving through their mornings with purpose rather than sitting still.

If you want something more structured, Ten Percent Happier runs $99/year. The walking curriculum is well-paced and the instructors are practical — less "surrender to the universe," more actual technique.

Happy to pull specific session recommendations from either app if that helps you decide.

Best,
Megan

Megan,

Tried Insight Timer Friday morning. 20-minute body scan session. Got through the whole thing without checking my phone, which at 6:30 CT on a Friday is genuinely impressive.

The $99 for Ten Percent Happier is fine — that's a rounding error against what I'm carrying in monthly rent across three locations. Will run both in parallel for two weeks and see which one I'm still using.

Quick turnaround. Appreciate it.

— Cecilia

Megan,

You mentioned that place on Washington Street last month — the one with the oat milk lattes. Finally tried it Tuesday after my riverwalk and ordered the same thing three days running, which I never do. Almond croissant was $6.50, latte $7, so $13.50 before tip every morning is not nothing, but it's earning it.

Thinking about making it the permanent stop before I open the Main Street salon. Worth the ten-minute detour or am I being impractical?

— Cecilia

Mom,

Three days in a row is basically a signed lease — you don't repeat anything that fast unless it's already decided. And if it sits right on the tail end of your riverwalk, the detour is barely a detour. I'd call that a routine, not a question.

Also, $13.50 is not nothing, but it's also not the thing in your budget that should be keeping you up at night.

Love,
Megan

Megan,

I didn't ask for budget context, but noted. The $14,500 in monthly rent across three locations is already doing plenty of talking without the croissant chiming in. The latte stays.

Mentioned it to Sharon and she's been walking that stretch of the river anyway, so now we're meeting there Thursdays. Figured two birds. She didn't hesitate, which means she's been looking for a reason to get out of the house before eight.

— Cecilia

Mom,

Obviously you turned a coffee recommendation into a standing Thursday appointment. That's the most you thing I've ever heard and I mean that with full respect.

If the walk and the latte and Sharon all line up before the salon opens, that's a genuinely good morning — don't let yourself talk yourself out of it by March. Tell Sharon I said hi, and save me an almond croissant for whenever I'm next in Naperville.

Love,
Megan

Megan,

The UV lamp display at station three still hasn't been restocked — I flagged this Tuesday and walked in this morning to the same empty shelf. I do the riverwalk specifically so I arrive at Main Street calm. That lasted about four minutes.

Main Street pulls the highest foot traffic of all three locations. We move $8K in product through that floor on a good week. Empty shelves aren't ambiance, they're lost sales.

Need confirmation by noon CT that the Gellen and OPI restock order has been placed. Also, the scheduling board had three open Thursday slots that never pushed to the app — corrected before 10 a.m. tomorrow, please.

— Cecilia
Golden Prairie Nails | Naperville

Cecilia,

Apologies for the gap — the Gellen and OPI restock order was placed at 10:47 a.m. CT this morning, with delivery confirmed for Wednesday before noon. That confirmation should have reached you Tuesday; it didn't, and I own that.

The three Thursday scheduling gaps have been corrected in the app as of 9:22 a.m. I also ran a full cross-check across all three locations for the remainder of the week and found no additional sync issues. I'll flag immediately if anything surfaces.

Going forward, I'll send a daily opening-prep status by 8 a.m. CT so you have it in hand before you arrive at Main Street.

— Megan
Client Support | Golden Prairie Nails

Megan,

Still waiting on the resolution from last Tuesday's ticket. My Gmail API integration stopped pulling client appointment confirmations into the main salon inbox — I'm manually checking three separate inboxes every morning before I even open the Main Street doors, and that's not a workflow I'm willing to sustain.

The OAuth token refresh is failing silently. No error, no bounce notification — emails just stop syncing somewhere around 6 a.m. CT. I need this resolved before the weekend rush hits all three locations.

— Cecilia Burnett
Golden Prairie Nails | Naperville, IL

Hi Cecilia,

Thank you for following up — I apologize for the delay on this.

I've pulled up your ticket and identified the root cause. Your OAuth 2.0 refresh token is expiring after 7 days because your Google Cloud project is still set to 'Testing' status. In that mode, Google automatically invalidates tokens on a rolling basis. Publishing the app to production will generate a persistent, non-expiring token and resolve the silent sync failures.

I've attached a step-by-step walkthrough. The process runs about 15 minutes end-to-end. Once complete, your Gmail API should resume syncing within the hour — no credential re-entry needed on your end. If anything looks off during the steps, reply here or let me know and I can set up a screen-share at your convenience.

Best,
Megan Burnett
Technical Support

Megan,

Done. Took 11 minutes, not 15. Sync is running clean across all three locations as of 7:44 a.m. CT — confirmed in the Main Street inbox before the doors opened.

One note for your documentation: your guide says to configure the OAuth scopes before publishing, but the Console had already locked them from the initial setup. Threw me for about 90 seconds. Worth flagging.

Appreciate the turnaround.

— Cecilia

Megan,

Still waiting on resolution from last Tuesday's ticket. We have three locations running on separate Google accounts and the Calendar API is only pulling appointment blocks from Main Street into the unified view — the other two aren't syncing at all. I open Main Street at 9 a.m. CT and by 9:15 my front desk is fielding questions about slots showing available that are already booked. A missed booking is a missed $65 service, minimum.

Need this closed before Friday.

— Cecilia Burnett
Golden Prairie Nails

Hi Cecilia,

Thank you for following up — I reviewed your ticket and I believe I've identified the problem. When the OAuth consent screen was completed, authorization was only granted for the account associated with Main Street. The other two locations were never included in that auth flow, so their calendar data simply isn't being requested.

To resolve this, each additional account needs to complete its own OAuth authorization for the Calendar API scope. I'll send a step-by-step walkthrough — it runs about five minutes per account. Could you confirm the email addresses tied to the other two locations so I can tailor the instructions?

Best,
Megan Burnett
Customer Support

Megan,

Addresses are goldenprairieoakbrook@gmail.com and goldenprairieroute59@gmail.com. Both are plain Gmail — not Workspace — which is probably your problem right there. Set them up before I knew the difference and never migrated.

If Workspace is what this integration actually requires, give me the cost and a realistic timeline. I'm already carrying $14,500 a month in rent across three locations. I'd like to know whether this is a $20 fix or a project.

— Cecilia

Hi Cecilia,

That's exactly it. The Google Calendar API's multi-account OAuth flow requires Google Workspace accounts — standard Gmail accounts can't be added as service principals in the same organization scope, which is why Main Street works and the other two don't.

Google Workspace Business Starter is $7 per user per month. For two locations, assuming 2–3 staff accounts each, you're looking at roughly $28–$42/month added. Migration from Gmail to Workspace typically completes within 24–48 hours with no interruption to existing calendar data. Once both accounts are migrated, re-authorization through the API takes under 30 minutes total and I can walk you through it live.

I can have a migration checklist to you by end of day today. Let me know if you'd prefer to schedule a call this week instead.

Best,
Megan Burnett
Customer Support

Megan,

Still waiting on the Drive API credentials from last week's ticket. I've got client records across three locations — Main Street, Ogden, and the Naperville Crossing spot — and none of them are syncing to the shared folder since the OAuth token expired.

This is a Tuesday morning problem and I'm already at the salon doing opening prep. I don't have time to chase a support thread while I'm getting three techs set up. What I need is the new client_secret.json pushed before 11 AM Central so my manager has it before the afternoon rush.

If there's a billing issue blocking the API quota increase, the account is under golden.prairie.nails@gmail.com and we're on the Business Standard plan — $18 per seat, 6 seats. Figure it out and let me know.

Cecilia Burnett
Golden Prairie Nails

Hi Cecilia,

Apologies for the delay — I've pulled up your account and confirmed the OAuth token expiration on your end. I've reissued the credentials and the new client_secret.json is now attached to this email. Please have your manager replace the existing file in the /config directory and re-run the authorization flow.

The API quota increase you requested (from 1,000 to 10,000 requests/day) has been approved and is active as of 9:47 AM Central. You should see all three location folders syncing within the next 15–20 minutes after reauthorization.

If anything doesn't resolve by noon, reply directly to this thread and I'll escalate to Tier 2 immediately — no need to open a new ticket.

Best,
Megan Burnett
Google Workspace Support

Megan,

Still waiting on resolution from the Square API ticket I opened Tuesday. The webhook delays are hitting all three locations — Main Street, Ogden, and the Route 59 shop — and I'm seeing a $340 gap between what Square is reporting and what actually hit the register at close last night. That's not rounding error.

I need this escalated before the weekend. Saturday alone runs $4,200 in card transactions across the three stores. I can't have reconciliation falling apart going into our busiest stretch.

— Cecilia Burnett
Golden Prairie Nails

Hi Cecilia,

Thank you for the follow-up. I've escalated your ticket (case #SQ-2847193) to our Tier 2 integration team and flagged it as high priority given the multi-location scope. The webhook delay you're describing is likely tied to an endpoint misconfiguration on the location-level API keys — each of your three locations needs its own key scoped to the correct location ID, and we're seeing instances where the main account key is being used instead, which causes reporting lag and occasional transaction mismatch.

I'm attaching a step-by-step remediation guide for your developer or whoever manages your Square dashboard. If you'd like, I can also schedule a 20-minute screen-share walkthrough for tomorrow morning — just let me know what time works on your end.

Best,
Megan Burnett
Square Seller Support

Megan,

The location-key issue tracks — we migrated to the third location in March and I doubt whoever set it up duplicated the config correctly. I'll have my IT guy pull the dashboard tonight.

Tomorrow morning works. I'm at the Main Street location by 8:15 CT and I'm heads-down on opening prep until 9:00, so anytime after 9 is fine. Send the calendar invite to this address. If the $340 discrepancy from last night can't be reconstructed once the keys are fixed, I'll need documentation for my accountant — just flag that now so it doesn't become a separate ticket later.

— Cecilia

Megan,

Tried that alarm app you sent over. Set it for 5:45, went with the 'sunrise simulation' — it's $4.99 a month to not be jolted awake, which, fine. Got my riverwalk in by 6:20, latte by 7:10, and hit Main Street for opening prep right on schedule. I'll admit the slow light is better than the foghorn I had before.

Still not sold on the 'sleep score.' Apparently I averaged 6.2 hours last week. I already knew that.

— Mom

Mom,

I knew you'd eventually cave on the app. The sleep score isn't there to shame you — it's actually useful once you look at trends over a few weeks. You might start noticing patterns around your heavier salon days.

Also, 6.2 hours is low, especially with three locations running. Are you actually finishing the full riverwalk each morning or cutting it short when you have early opens?

Love you,
Megan

Megan,

Full walk, every morning. Non-negotiable. That's my $0 therapy and nobody's cutting into it.

I did look at the trend graph. Tuesday is my worst sleep night, apparently — which tracks. Monday closes late and I'm back at Main Street by 7:30 Tuesday for the vendor delivery. $14,500 in monthly rent across three locations doesn't exactly quiet the mind at 10 p.m. I'll try for the hours. No promises on the week of the 15th.

— Mom

Mom,

Tuesday being the worst makes complete sense. Your brain doesn't just switch off after a late close and a delivery window at dawn.

One thing that actually helps: hold the same wake time on Sunday too, so your rhythm doesn't reset going into the week. I know that sounds like something I'd say and you'd ignore, but it works. And maybe let Sharon cover the Tuesday open once in a while — she can handle it.

You're doing great. Just let the app do a little of the work.

Love,
Megan

Megan,

Still no resolution on the audio sync issue I reported June 17th. I'm on the riverwalk by 6:10 most mornings and the guided session keeps dropping the cue track after the first two minutes — happens on both the iPhone and the iPad, so it's not a device problem.

I run three nail salons in Naperville. I don't have twenty minutes to chase a support ticket for an $89 annual subscription. Fix it or process the refund.

Cecilia Burnett

Hi Cecilia,

Thank you for following up — your ticket has been open past our 48-hour SLA and I apologize for that delay. We identified a caching issue with the guided audio cue track earlier this week, and a patch rolled out this morning in version 4.2.1. Updating the app should resolve the drop you've been experiencing on your morning walks.

Once you've updated, if the session still cuts out after the opening cues, reply here and I'll pull your device logs and escalate directly to the engineering team — no new ticket, no hold queue.

— Megan Burnett
Member Support, Stillwater Wellness

Megan,

Following up on ticket #4471 — the oat milk substitution on my standing Tuesday order still hasn't been applied. Third week in a row. I pick that order up on my way into the Main Street location before 7:40 CT, so I don't have a window to flag it at the counter.

If the system can't hold a modifier on a recurring order, just say so and I'll deal with it another way. But I'd like a straight answer before Tuesday.

— Cecilia Burnett
Golden Prairie Nails

Hi Cecilia,

I apologize for the repeated miss — I pulled your account and found the modifier wasn't saving correctly due to a sync issue affecting recurring orders placed before our March platform update. It slipped through quality checks longer than it should have.

I've manually applied the oat milk substitution to all future Tuesday orders and added a $12 credit to your account to cover the three affected orders. You should see it within 24–48 hours. I've also flagged your account so a confirmation note will appear in your order summary each week — that way you can verify it's set before you're out the door.

Thank you for your patience, and please don't hesitate to reach out.

Megan Burnett | Customer Support

Megan,

Credit posted, and Tuesday's order finally had the right milk. Three weeks is a long time to pay for something I wasn't getting, but I appreciate you fixing it.

The confirmation note still isn't showing in the summary. I run three locations and my mornings don't have a lot of margin — I'd rather see it there and not need it than discover it's missing at 6:55 when I'm already on the riverwalk.

— Cecilia

Megan,

Opening prep at Main Street ran long this morning — the supply delivery was 22 minutes late and two of the UV lamps in station 3 are flickering. I need you to contact the vendor and get a replacement timeline by end of day Wednesday, Central Time.

Also, the product inventory sheet from last week still shows the OPI Bubble Bath shortage as 'pending.' That's been pending since the 14th. I need a confirmed restock date, not a status.

Cecilia
Golden Prairie Nails — Main Street

Hi Cecilia,

Apologies for the delay on both. I just got off the phone with the lamp vendor — they can have two replacement UV units to Main Street by Friday morning before your 9 a.m. open. I'll have them confirm in writing by tonight.

On the OPI Bubble Bath, the distributor is showing a warehouse backorder through July 3rd. I've flagged it as priority and requested expedited shipping. I'll send you the confirmation number as soon as it comes through. Is there a substitute shade you'd want stocked in the meantime?

Megan
Golden Prairie Nails — Support

Megan,

Friday works, but I need the delivery before 8:30 — not 9. My opening prep window is tight and I'm not stopping a lamp install mid-walkthrough.

No substitute on the Bubble Bath. Clients book specifically for it. If we're out through July 3rd, update the online booking notes so nobody shows up expecting a shade we can't deliver. That's a $0 fix that saves a real conversation.

Cecilia

Cecilia,

Updated the delivery window to 8:30 a.m. — vendor confirmed. I've also added a temporary note to the Main Street booking page flagging the OPI Bubble Bath availability gap through July 3rd. It's live now.

One more thing: I noticed the morning riverwalk schedule means you're usually pulling into Main Street right around 7:50. If anything changes with the Friday delivery window, I'll text you directly so you're not walking in cold. Let me know if there's anything else you need before the week closes out.

Megan
Golden Prairie Nails — Support
Client called about a chipped gel set from Main Street — offered a complimentary fix this week. Rebooked.
Rebooking request for Saturday at Orchard Plaza. All gel-x slots are full; offered the following Tuesday.
Follow-up on the Wheaton appointment-reminder delay. Twilio confirmed the send window is fixed now.
Gift-card balance question resolved — client's remaining balance applied at Main Street checkout.
Complaint about wait time at Orchard Plaza on Saturday. Apologized, added a $5 credit to her profile.
New-client inquiry from the website chat about lash services at Wheaton — sent the launch timeline.
Requested a manager callback at Main Street about a group booking for eight. Amanda Keller following up.
